edges.description: Antibody cross-reactivity with connexin 26 in the inner ear contributes to cochleovestibular injury.
Antibody binding to endothelial antigens promotes endothelial injury and systemic vasculitis.
Cochlear sensory epithelial injury produces sensorineural hearing loss.
Vestibular sensory injury produces rotational vertigo.
Cochlear injury produces tinnitus.
Progressive vestibular sensory loss produces bilateral vestibular hypofunction.
Systemic immune-mediated vascular inflammation manifests as clinically apparent vasculitis.
Vascular inflammation can involve the aorta and other large vessels.
Vascular inflammation can involve medium-caliber arteries.
Arterial wall inflammation, including coronary arteritis, can occur.
Aortic-wall inflammation is a large-vessel manifestation of systemic vasculitis.
Vasculitic involvement of retinal vessels in atypical ocular disease.
Inflammation of episcleral vasculature as an ocular manifestation.
Ocular immune-mediated inflammation manifesting as uveitis in atypical disease.
Diffuse intraocular immune-mediated inflammation manifesting as panuveitis.
Aortic-root and valve involvement from aortitis can lead to aortic regurgitation.
Coronary stenosis from coronaritis can precipitate acute coronary syndrome.
Coronary arteritis can produce coronary artery stenosis.

nodes.description: The proposed initiating mechanism is post-infectious molecular mimicry. An immunodominant peptide recognized by IgG from Cogan syndrome patients shares sequence similarity with the reovirus III major core protein and the SSA/Ro autoantigen, breaking immune tolerance and generating autoantibodies that cross-react with inner-ear and endothelial self-antigens (including CD148/DEP-1 and connexin 26). Infection may contribute, but autoimmunity is regarded as the primary driver; the pathogenesis remains incompletely defined.
